settings.heading: When do your breakouts usually flare up?
Where on your face do they show up most?
Have you tried any of these?
Which of these also sounds familiar?
Your skin is on a hormonal clock
settings.paragraph: Breakouts that arrive on schedule (the week before your period) are the textbook hormonal-acne pattern. Estrogen fluctuations drive sebum, inflammation, and chin/jaw breakouts. DIM is formulated to support how your body metabolizes estrogen — which is why cyclical breakouts are exactly the use-case it's built for.
settings.micro: Most users notice the first shift within 2–4 weeks of consistent use.

settings.heading: When topicals aren't enough — look inside
settings.paragraph: If retinoids, antibiotics, and cleansers keep failing, the trigger is likely INTERNAL — not on the surface of your skin. Persistent adult breakouts almost always trace back to the hormonal pathway: DIM supports the estrogen metabolism that topical treatments can't touch.
settings.micro: Full effect typically takes 60–90 days. Stay consistent.

settings.heading: Stress messes with your hormones — and your skin
settings.paragraph: Cortisol from chronic stress raises androgen activity, which boosts sebum and inflammation. The result: breakouts that flare during your hardest weeks. DIM supports the hormone balance that stress most reliably disrupts.
settings.micro: Pair DIM with sleep + magnesium for the strongest results.

settings.heading: Random isn't always random — there's usually a pattern underneath
settings.paragraph: What looks random often masks a cycle-linked or stress-linked pattern that becomes visible only after a few months of consistent observation. DIM is a 60-day commitment that helps reveal — and improve — the pattern beneath.
settings.micro: Keep a quick skin journal for the first 8 weeks — patterns will emerge.

settings.h2: Feel like yourself again — clear skin from the inside out.
settings.image_alt: Calm woman with clear skin, eyes closed at golden hour

settings.heading: 1. The Problem
settings.body: Your skin reflects your hormones. When estrogen builds up faster than your body can break it down, some of that excess gets converted into 4-hydroxyestrone — a metabolite linked in research to inflammation, increased sebum production, and breakouts along the jawline and chin.

settings.heading: 2. The Science
settings.body: DIM (diindolylmethane) is a compound naturally found in cruciferous vegetables like broccoli and cauliflower. Clinical research shows DIM helps the body convert excess estrogen into its gentler form (2-hydroxyestrone) — supporting the hormonal pathway behind adult acne.1
settings.callout_text: 📚 Backed by decades of peer-reviewed research on DIM and estrogen metabolism.

settings.heading: 3. The Catch
settings.body: The problem? You'd need to eat pounds of raw broccoli every day to get a meaningful dose of DIM. One 4WELL capsule delivers 370mg — paired with BioPerine®, the patented black-pepper extract studied for enhanced absorption.2

settings.body: I'm 34 and had cystic acne since my 20s. Nothing topical ever worked for me. Started DIM in August, by November I had way fewer breakouts. Telling all my friends.

settings.name: Jordan W.

settings.question: How fast will I see results?
settings.answer: Many women notice initial changes within 2–4 weeks. Most see fuller results around weeks 6–8 of consistent daily use. For full hormonal balance, plan on 3 months. Individual results vary.

settings.question: Are there any side effects?
settings.answer: DIM is generally well-tolerated. Some women report mild headaches or darker urine in the first week — both are normal as your body adjusts to estrogen metabolism. If symptoms feel unusual, stop and consult your doctor.

settings.question: Can I take DIM with birth control or other medications?
settings.answer: Talk to your doctor first. DIM can affect how your body processes certain medications, including birth control. Don't combine without medical guidance.

settings.question: Is it safe for perimenopause and menopause?
settings.answer: Yes — DIM supports estrogen balance at any life stage. Many women in their 40s and 50s use it for perimenopause symptoms.

settings.question: How many capsules per day?
settings.answer: One capsule per day with a meal. The 30-capsule bottle is a 1-month supply.

settings.question: What if it doesn't work for me?
settings.answer: You're protected by a 30-day money-back guarantee. Email customer service for a full refund. Refund processed within 5 business days of receiving the bottle.

settings.question: Where is it made?
settings.answer: 4WELL DIM is manufactured in a GMP-certified facility in the USA. Each batch is third-party lab tested for purity, potency, and heavy metals. COA available on request.
